title: Wet-felted flower brooch
subtitle: A small five-petal flower in merino, attached to a brooch pin
excerpt: Wet-felt a small five-petal flower by fulling individual petal shapes and joining them at a central button of contrast roving. The flower is about 5 cm across and takes under an hour to make. It is a useful first project for learning controlled fulling of small shapes and how to join separately felted pieces.
sourceNotes: Wet-felted flower brooches are a well-established introductory project in wet-felting workshops. The five-petal form with separate petals joined at a centre is a standard teaching construction.

content.text: is made by rolling about 2 g of merino roving into a small oval and fulling it in the palm with hot soapy water. The five petals are then arranged around a 1 g centre button and joined with a
before the whole assembly is felted together in a last rinse. Brooch pin attached with a few hand stitches through the centre.

items.name: Hot water and cold water access

content.text: For each petal, take about 2 g of roving, wet it thoroughly in hot soapy water, and roll it in the palm of your hand in small circular strokes. As the fibres lock, press the forming mass into a flat oval with your thumb. Continue rolling and pressing until each petal is firm and holds its oval shape without pressing. Make five petals. Rinse each in cold water and press out moisture.
Repeat with 2 g of contrast roving, this time rolling into a compact round button about 1.5 cm across. Full more firmly than the petals so the centre is denser.

Arrange the five petals in a circle on the foam mat, tips pointing outward, with their inner ends overlapping at the centre by about 1 cm. Place the centre button over the inner ends. Needle straight down through the centre button into the petals below with a size 38 needle, working around the circumference of the button until it is firmly locked to all five petals.

Wet the assembled flower briefly in warm water and press it flat on the mat. Leave to dry. Once dry, sew the brooch pin to the back of the centre button using waxed thread, passing the thread through the felt and catching both pin loops securely.

items.fix: Weigh each petal portion before felting: 2 g per petal gives consistent results.
items.cause: Roving amounts were not consistent between petals

items.fix: Work additional needle strokes through the centre button into each petal, penetrating both layers fully.
items.cause: Insufficient needling at the centre-petal join
items.problem: Centre button detaches from petals with wear

items.fix: Stop fulling each petal as soon as it holds its shape. Over-fulled felt loses the slight dome that reads as a petal.
items.problem: The flower is too flat and stiff
